item.title: Pretty Cool
item.content:encoded: This is a really cool idea! I also like the whole thing of it being a garbage dump. Plus, i'm always a sucker for passive-aggressive SCPs

item.title: A soul in a garbage dump
item.content:encoded:
This is very original, and quite creative. Well done.
I'd love to know more about the tests, how does it dissapear (immediatly from sight or does it need to be closed, etc) and what happens when there is absolutely no crevasse or place to put the thing.
Also what kind of objects do they put monthly to appease the dump since they will most likely go to D-class? Wouldn't it also make more sense to contain it in a 2Km area where there is no D-class or other SCPs?
Again loved this idea, I just feel that if extended it could be much better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Won't 140 cause some sort of breach though

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
◾SCP-140

You know what might be interesting? A new entry in SCP-140 as a result of it leaving containment due to SCP-3110.

item.title: Re: Alrighty, First SCP!
item.content:encoded:
Original source of image: http://www.holoweb.net/liam/pictures/2004-07-montreal/pages/cimg1684/

Image is under public domain.

item.title: Alrighty, First SCP!
item.content:encoded:
After much internal debate, I decided it was time to post this puppy. Hopefully, you guys enjoy!

I just hope it doesn't get downvoted into oblivion. Only time will tell.

ORIGINAL IMAGE TAKEN FROM: http://www.holoweb.net/liam/pictures/2004-07-montreal/pages/cimg1684/
THANKS:
bittermixin (Drafts and Critiques)
Sync (#site19)
Tuomey (#site17)
salvagebar (#site17)
Scented_Shadow (#site17)
/u/MDCLXV1665 (/r/SCP)
Waterfire (#site19)
SleepyIsHollow (#site19)
djkaktus (#site19)
